feed.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes

entry.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
entry.summary:
Michael Folkson, in his communication, articulates a firm support for the Taproot activation alongside any initiatives aimed at facilitating its implementation. He emphasizes his staunch opposition to any User Activated Soft Fork (UASF) release that is not compatible with a Core Segregated Witness (ST) release, highlighting a preference for projects that navigate around finalized ST parameters within Core as a preparation for a potential, albeit unlikely, failure of ST deployment. This stance underscores a broader principle of consistency which Folkson favors over inconsistency, albeit acknowledging the latter as a legitimate perspective.
Folkson also addresses a contradiction regarding his previously stated preference for a fully height-based design approach, noting the peculiar reversal of this stance within a span of six days. The discussion further extends into the realm of marketing strategies associated with UASF client launches, where Folkson expresses reservations about making technical decisions influenced by speculative marketing strategies. He warns of the risks associated with allowing marketing narratives around concepts like "world computers" and "Turing completeness" to dictate the integration of pull requests, suggesting such a path could lead to undesirable outcomes.
The dialogue is enriched with references to GitHub discussions ([link](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21377issuecomment-802396191)), providing a tangible context to Folkson's points. Contact details and cryptographic keys are provided, reinforcing the professional nature of the communication and Folkson's openness to further dialogue on these technical matters.
